persons.arguments_against: Counter-hypotheses: (1) the dossier flags the namesake trap and there are 4 Deverell officials, so a different C. Deverell (e.g. a brother) could be the Windward Islands or Mauritius governor; (2) the Windward stint fails colony/tenure/position checks entirely, suggesting the bio never served there. Both fail on the verbatim text. The biography explicitly reads 'gov. and c.in-c., Wind Is., 1955; Maur., 1959-62' — the deterministic colony FAIL for the Windward stint is a parser artifact (the timeline mis-inherited 'Jamaica' for that clause, and mislabeled the Mauritius clause's position). The Windward staff lists show 'C. M. Deverell, C.M.G.' in 1955-56 becoming 'Sir Colville Deverell, K.C.M.G.' in 1957 — exactly tracking the biography's C.M.G. (1955) and K.C.M.G. (1957); the Mauritius lists show 'Sir Colville Montgomery Deverell, K.C.M.G.' as Governor 1960-61, matching 'Maur., 1959-62' and the 1953 entry's initials 'C. M.'. The rare forename Colville, the unique governorships, the matching honour progression across both stints, and the seamless career hand-off (Windward ends 1957, Mauritius 1959-62) leave no room for a namesake. Despite the single-initial warning, the bio carries the full distinctive forename 'Colville', and the assignments rest on office + place + dates + honours, not name alone.
persons.would_overturn: Evidence of a second Colville Deverell in colonial service in the 1950s-60s, or records showing different persons held the Windward Islands governorship 1955-57 and the Mauritius governorship 1960-62.

notes: The deterministic FAILs on the Windward stint (colony, tenure, position, place_inherited) are all artifacts of the timeline parser inheriting 'Jamaica' for the 'gov. and c.in-c., Wind Is., 1955' clause and attaching the Wind Is position string to the Mauritius clause; the verbatim entry states both postings explicitly. Honour progression (C.M.G. 1955 -> K.C.M.G. 1957) is independently visible in the Windward staff-list rows.
